steps.title: Identify the conclusion
steps.content: Martinez and colleagues concluded that H. ochraceiceps associates the alarm calls with danger. To support this, we need evidence that the freezing/scattering reaction is specifically tied to the alarm calls — not to other stimuli or random behavior.

steps.title: Match a control finding
steps.content:
Choice A says when researchers played random control noise, the birds had no reaction. That rules out the rival explanation that the birds react to any sound, isolating the alarm call as the trigger and supporting the danger-association conclusion.

B (other species reacting) and C (consistency across individuals) say nothing about whether H. ochraceiceps specifically links the calls to danger. D actively undermines the conclusion by showing the birds froze without any call at all.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
